title: Caterpillar stitch binding
materials: text paper 90 gsm, grain short, folded into 4-sheet signatures
book-cloth or decorative paper for spine wrap
sources: Keith Smith, Non-Adhesive Binding (1990) — exposition of chain-link exposed-spine structures; cross-reference with Coptic-stitch tradition documented in Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901).
notes: The caterpillar stitch uses a chain-link stitch that alternates between signatures with a visible link on the outside of the spine — different from the standard Coptic chain (which links only at the kettle stations). Named for the caterpillar-like ridge of links running down the spine. Covers: sewing station spacing, the link move, keeping even tension across all signatures. No boards required but optional soft boards shown.
